home.title: Community Environmental Risk Advisor
home.heroTitle: Understand the environmental risks of a mining project
home.heroSubtitle: Select a location on the map or choose a case study, fill in project details, and get an independent environmental risk assessment in minutes.

mapInput.searchPlaceholder: Search for a city or town...
mapInput.additionalDetails: Add more details to improve your assessment
mapInput.additionalDetailsDescription: The more you share, the more specific your risk assessment will be. All fields are optional.

caseStudy.title: Sample Case Studies
caseStudy.description: Select a mining project to explore
caseStudy.mapHint: Or click anywhere on the map to place your own marker

assessment.title: Community Environmental Review
assessment.projectNotFound: Project not found. Please start a new assessment.
assessment.shareDescription: Share functionality will allow you to collaborate with community members and advisors.
assessment.downloadDescription: Download full assessment report as PDF with all data and recommendations.

riskOverview.criticalAlert: {{count}} critical risk{{plural}} identified that require immediate attention. These issues could significantly impact community wellbeing and environment.
riskOverview.verificationAlert: This assessment is based on publicly available data and should be verified with local knowledge and current conditions.
riskOverview.envRiskDescription: Independent findings based on publicly available data and environmental standards — with solutions for each issue
riskOverview.disclaimerText: Assessments use publicly available global datasets (WRI Aqueduct, Global Forest Watch, LandMark, and others) and may not reflect local conditions. We recommend consulting local environmental experts and community knowledge to validate findings.

actionPlanner.title: Your Action Plan
actionPlanner.description: Know your rights and what actions to take at each project phase
actionPlanner.currentPhaseAlert: Current Phase: {{name}} - This is a critical time for community action. Review the actions below and ensure your community is prepared.
actionPlanner.checklistDescription: Download a printable checklist for this phase with all actions and documents needed.
actionPlanner.workshopDescription: Schedule a community workshop to prepare for this project phase together.
actionPlanner.documentEverythingDesc: Keep records of all communications, meetings, and observations
actionPlanner.actCollectivelyDesc: Unite with neighboring communities for stronger negotiating position
actionPlanner.knowYourRightsDesc: Understand legal protections before entering any agreements
actionPlanner.seekIndependentDesc: Don't rely solely on company-provided information
actionPlanner.planLongTermDesc: Consider impacts beyond the mining project's lifespan

learn.title: Learn About Mining & Your Rights
learn.description: Educational articles and practical documents to help your community understand environmental risks, know your rights, and take action.
learn.documentsDescription: Downloadable templates, checklists, and guides based on real community needs identified through interviews across the US, Indonesia, and DRC.
learn.whyMatters: Why This Matters for Your Community

visualizations.title: Interactive Data Visualizations
visualizations.description: Explore environmental data and project impacts through interactive maps and charts

waterRisk.methodologyText: This analysis uses WRI Aqueduct 4.0 baseline water stress data, combined with projected mining water demand based on ore processing requirements and regional evaporation rates. Raster visualization shows 5km resolution water risk indicators. Drought projections incorporate IPCC climate scenarios.
waterRisk.riverNote: River network data sourced from HydroRIVERS (WWF HydroSHEDS), showing stream order classification and estimated discharge.
waterRisk.stressFinding3: 3 communities compete for same water source

landBio.kbaOverlap: Mine footprint overlaps {{kba}}% with KBA and {{protected}}% with protected areas
landBio.indigenousOverlap: {{pct}}% of concession area intersects indigenous/community lands
landBio.fpicDescription: Free, Prior and Informed Consent (FPIC) has not been initiated for any of the {{count}} affected communities. This is required under ILO Convention 169 and the UN Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples (UNDRIP).
landBio.fpicRequired: FPIC must be obtained before any project activities begin on indigenous or community lands.
landBio.methodologyText: Protected area boundaries from the World Database on Protected Areas (WDPA, Protected Planet). Key Biodiversity Areas identified by the BirdLife/KBA Partnership. Indigenous and community land tenure from LandMark (WRI), licensed CC BY-SA 4.0. Overlap percentages are calculated against the mining concession boundary. FPIC status is based on publicly available consultation records. Geological deposit locations sourced from the USGS Mineral Resources Data System (MRDS), a global database of metallic and nonmetallic mineral resources. All data should be verified with local communities and relevant authorities.

impactRadius.communityImpactDesc: This visualization shows estimated impacts based on typical mining operations of this scale. Actual impacts depend on mitigation measures, local topography, and operational practices. Communities should request detailed impact assessments for their specific areas.

comparison.compareDescription: How this project compares to regional and global averages
comparison.similarDescription: Historical outcomes from comparable mining operations
comparison.keyInsightText: Projects with similar characteristics to yours have faced significant challenges. Early community organization and independent monitoring have been critical success factors for protecting community interests.

sources.title: Sources & Methodology
sources.citationText: Estimated using the WRI Environmental Risk Advisor based on publicly available global datasets. Assessment generated for {{name}}. Data confidence levels and limitations are noted in the full assessment. We recommend supplementing with local expert review.
sources.confidenceHigh: Based on multiple, recent, peer-reviewed or government-verified datasets with strong local calibration. Suitable for formal citations.
sources.confidenceMedium: Based on reputable global datasets that may not reflect all local conditions. Recommended to verify with local experts before citing in formal proceedings.
sources.confidenceLow: Based on limited or outdated data, or inferred from regional patterns. Use as a starting point only — independent verification is strongly recommended.
sources.waterRiskDesc: Water risk scores use the WRI Aqueduct 4.0 framework combining baseline water stress, inter-annual variability, seasonal depletion, and groundwater table decline. Where available, local basin studies provide calibrated adjustments for arid-region salars and confined aquifers.
sources.biodiversityHabitatDesc: Biodiversity assessments overlay project footprints against Key Biodiversity Areas (KBAs), IUCN protected area categories, and species range maps from the IBAT Alliance. Proximity to critical habitats and documented endangered species observations are factored into the risk level.
sources.communityDisplacementDesc: Displacement risk is estimated using community mapping data, settlement proximity analysis, and population density within projected impact zones. Household counts and village boundaries are cross-referenced with satellite imagery and census records.
sources.indigenousTerritoryDesc: Indigenous land overlap is assessed using LandMark global platform data on indigenous and community lands, supplemented by national land registries and local tenure records where available. FPIC status is tracked when disclosed.
sources.foodSecurityDesc: Food security impact is estimated from agricultural census data, satellite-derived land use classification, and crop productivity indices. The assessment considers the share of arable land within the project area relative to the surrounding district.

solutions.title: Mitigation Solutions
solutions.subtitle: Constructive actions that communities, companies, and government can take to reduce environmental risks. Use these to prepare for consultations and negotiations.
solutions.connectExpertsDescription: Connect with independent environmental, legal, and community development experts who can provide in-depth, non-automated advice for your specific situation.
